parameters.name: vdd
parameters.override_behavior: sets the voltage-coded high level for binary and valid outputs

parameters.name: vth
parameters.override_behavior: sets the decision threshold for every thermometer input

parameters.name: tr
parameters.override_behavior: sets output rise and fall smoothing

evaluation_summary.full_credit: valid candidate and reference pass and all five negatives killed behaviorally

identity.supported_scope: pure voltage-domain event-driven transient behavioral Verilog-A
identity.title: Thermometer To Binary Encoder 8b

modeling_constraints: Preserve cumulative thermometer ordering from th[0] toward th[255].
Use smooth voltage contributions for all driven outputs.
Do not add state, undeclared artifacts, debug outputs, validation hooks, or simulator-specific side channels.

properties.observable_contract: valid is high exactly for prefix thermometer words representing counts 0 through 255: asserted inputs start at th[0], contain no low-to-high hole, and th[255] remains low; the all-low word is valid and the all-high 256-line word is invalid.
For a valid word, b[7:0] equals the number of asserted thermometer inputs, with b[7] the most significant bit and b[0] the least significant bit.
For any non-cumulative thermometer word, valid is low and every binary output bit is low.
The all-low word produces code 0, while th[0] through th[254] high and th[255] low produces code 255.
Binary and valid outputs use 0 V for logic low and vdd for logic high with finite transition smoothing.

security_summary: only declared ./dut source bindings are allowed
DUT redefinition, direct output drive, private hierarchical probes, arbitrary file access, and unbounded analyses are rejected
